output:
CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

content.text: Now wire `findPath()` to capture time + nodes and call Scorer:

content.content:
<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String:     function findPath() {
        if (!grid) return;
        abortSearch();
        View.clearFootprints && View.clearFootprints();
        View.clearPath && View.clearPath();
        operations = [];

        var algoId  = $('#algo_select').val();
        var finder  = buildFinder(algoId);
        if (!finder) { setStatus('Unknown algorithm: ' + algoId); return; }

        var cloned  = grid.clone();
        var path    = finder.findPath(startX, startY, endX, endY, cloned);
        var opsCopy = operations.slice();
        operations  = [];

        isSearching = true;
        setStatus('Searching…');

        var opIdx = 0;
        animTimer = setInterval(function() {
            if (opIdx >= opsCopy.length) {
                clearInterval(animTimer);
                isSearching = false;
                View.drawPath(path);
                if (path.length) {
                    setStatus('Path found · ' + path.length + ' nodes');
                } else {
                    setStatus('No path found');
                }
                return;
            }
            var op = opsCopy[opIdx++];
            if (View.supportedOperations.indexOf(op.attr) !== -1) {
                View.setAttributeAt(op.x, op.y, op.attr, op.value);
            }
        }, 1000 / opsPerSecond);
    }</tool_use_error>

content.thinking:
Good. Now each element appears only once:
- `#score_card` - once (line 239)
- `#comparison_drawer` - once (line 259)
- `#show_comparison` - once (line 231)
- `#clear_runs_btn` - once (line 263)
- `scorer.js` - once (line 298)
- `Scorer.captureRun` and `Scorer.showScoreCard` - in findPath (lines 479-480)


- `Scorer.init()` - at boot (line 627)

The CSS rules for `#score_card` won't fully apply since the inline-styled version doesn't have the expected HTML structure—there's no `<h3>` tag, just a `<strong>` instead—but that's fine since those selectors just won't match anything. Now I need to verify that the `showScoreCard` function in `scorer.js` can actually find the elements it's looking for using jQuery selectors like `.sc-algorithm`, `.sc-path-length`, `.sc-nodes`, and `.sc-time`.

Checking the existing score card markup against what the Scorer module expects, all the required class names are present: `.sc-algorithm`, `.sc-path-length`, `.sc-nodes`, `.sc-time`, `.sc-save-btn`, `.sc-saved-msg`, and `.sc-close` are all there. The comparison drawer also has the `<tbody>` and `#clear_runs_btn` that the `init` function needs.

The inline `display:none` styles on both elements are fine since the JavaScript uses jQuery's `.show()` and `.hide()` methods to toggle visibility, which will override those initial styles. I'm also noticing that the CSS block for `#score_card` defines `.sc-row` styling, but since that class doesn't actually exist in the markup, it just won't apply—no conflict there. jQuery's `.show()` method will properly override the inline `display:none` by setting the style property directly, so there shouldn't be any conflicts between the CSS and inline styles. The unused selectors won't cause issues either, so I should run the tests to verify everything works as expected.

content.text: Good - each element appears exactly once. Let me run the test suite to check no regressions:

content.text:
376/376 pass. All four integration issues fixed:

1. **`scorer.js` script tag** — already present (linter added it)
2. **Scorer DOM elements** — `#score_card`, `#comparison_drawer`, `#show_comparison`, `#clear_runs_btn` all added with required child elements (`.sc-algorithm`, `.sc-path-length`, `.sc-nodes`, `.sc-time`, `.sc-save-btn`, `.sc-saved-msg`, `.sc-close`)
3. **`findPath()` wired** — captures `timeMs` via `performance.now()`, counts `nodesExplored` from opened nodes, calls `Scorer.captureRun()` + `Scorer.showScoreCard()` after animation
4. **`Scorer.init()`** — called at boot (was already in the linter's version)

result:
376/376 pass. All four integration issues fixed:

1. **`scorer.js` script tag** — already present (linter added it)
2. **Scorer DOM elements** — `#score_card`, `#comparison_drawer`, `#show_comparison`, `#clear_runs_btn` all added with required child elements (`.sc-algorithm`, `.sc-path-length`, `.sc-nodes`, `.sc-time`, `.sc-save-btn`, `.sc-saved-msg`, `.sc-close`)
3. **`findPath()` wired** — captures `timeMs` via `performance.now()`, counts `nodesExplored` from opened nodes, calls `Scorer.captureRun()` + `Scorer.showScoreCard()` after animation
4. **`Scorer.init()`** — called at boot (was already in the linter's version)
